title: Home
text:
Business Studies Student | Business Analytics, Marketing Strategy & Process Improvement
I am a final-year Business Studies student at Dublin City University with academic interests in business analytics, marketing planning, and process improvement. Through university projects and internship experience, I have developed practical skills in business analysis, financial thinking, problem-solving, and professional communication.
About Me Projects CV

title: Home
text:
Welcome
This ePortfolio presents my academic background, project experience, and professional interests. It highlights my work in business analysis, marketing strategy, operational improvement, and customer-focused business development.

title: Reflection
text:
Reflection
Developing this portfolio has helped me think more critically about how academic work can be presented in a professional and accessible way. Rather than simply listing modules or assignments, I have had to reflect on what each experience says about my strengths, interests, and future direction.
Through building this portfolio, I have become more aware of the connection between business analysis, communication, and digital presentation. My academic projects, particularly in enterprise development and process improvement, showed me that strong ideas need to be supported by clear structure, evidence, and practical recommendations.
This process also encouraged me to think more carefully about my career direction. I am especially interested in roles that combine analytical thinking with business decision-making, including business analysis, marketing planning, and process improvement.

title: Projects
text:
Park & Polish was developed as part of a New Enterprise Development project focused on identifying a real customer pain point and transforming it into a scalable business concept. The idea centred on a mobile car care service delivered while customers’ vehicles were already parked in locations such as shopping centres, airports, and business parks.
This project explored convenience, sustainability, digital booking, partner-based growth, and customer-focused service design. It helped me strengthen my understanding of value proposition development, customer insight, and strategic marketing thinking.



This project involved a comprehensive business analysis of a cookie manufacturing company, with the objective of improving both production and sales processes. The work focused on identifying operational inefficiencies, calculating production costs, analysing profit margins, and evaluating opportunities to reduce costs across the supply chain.
By applying business analysis tools such as cost-benefit analysis and process mapping, the project developed practical recommendations to improve efficiency, reduce waste, and strengthen profitability.

title: About
text:
I am a final-year Business Studies student at Dublin City University, with a strong academic focus on business analytics, marketing planning, and commercial problem-solving. Alongside my studies, I am currently working as an Accounting Assistant Intern, where I support day-to-day finance and administrative processes while gaining practical experience in financial documentation, organisation, and business operations.
I am also pursuing ACCA studies, which are strengthening my understanding of accounting, finance, and professional practice. Through my education, internship experience, and project work, I have built a solid foundation in business analysis, structured thinking, and decision-making based on evidence and business objectives.
My main professional interests lie in business analysis, marketing strategy, process improvement, and graduate roles where I can continue to develop analytical, strategic, and communication skills. I am particularly interested in projects that combine commercial insight with practical recommendations to improve performance, customer value, and operational efficiency.



Dublin City University
Bachelor’s degree in Business Studies, with focus on Business Analytics and Marketing Planning
Rathmines College
QQI Level 5 in English (ESOL) and IT / Business
